level_signals: 3+ years experience in a product management role
Base pay $140K-$165K in Zone 1, consistent with mid-level PM at a fintech
No mention of managing other PMs or leading a team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years experience in a product management role
Familiarity using research, experimentation and product analytics to guide decision making
Ability to balance strategic with creative decision making
A strong sense of urgency and speed
High standards and a strong work ethic - you always bring your best and expect your colleagues to do the same
Familiarity with payment systems (ACH, wires, card networks) or vendor integrations such as data aggregators
Clear communication skills and ability to work effectively with engineering, operations, and external partners

scope.surface: Payment rails, vendor integrations (data aggregators, payment processors), deposits, withdrawals, and internal money movement infrastructure
core_responsibilities: Define product requirements for payment rails and vendor integrations, including data aggregators and payment processors
Partner with engineering to deliver reliable systems that support deposits, withdrawals, and internal money movement
Identify and prioritize improvements that reduce transaction failures, latency, and operational overhead
Work with compliance and legal teams to ensure all payment systems meet regulatory requirements
Monitor key metrics such as transaction success rates and processing times, and use insights to guide product decisions

compensation.equity_form: equity ownership mentioned as part of performance-driven compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed across three geographic zones
Clear product surface and ownership area (payment rails, vendor integrations)
Specific metrics called out (transaction success rates, processing times)
Equity, bonus, and 401(k) matching mentioned as part of total comp
Cross-functional scope is well-defined (engineering, compliance, legal, operations, external partners)
red_flags: Hybrid requirement is 3 days per week in Menlo Park or NYC — eliminates remote candidates
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
No mention of seniority level in title or requirements beyond '3+ years'; scope could be ambiguous
JD language is highly culture-fit-heavy ('high-performing', 'fast-moving', 'best work of their careers') which may signal high-pressure environment

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years required, not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from Robinhood's known product and JD context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; partially stated in JD but framed as monitoring, not targets)
archetype classification (Technical PM chosen due to payment infrastructure focus, but role has generalist PM characteristics)
ambiguities: The 'Familiarity with payment systems (ACH, wires, card networks) or vendor integrations such as data aggregators' requirement uses 'or', making it unclear whether both are required or either suffices. Treated as a single hard requirement.

The base pay range shown ($140K-$165K) is for Zone 1 (Menlo Park, CA; New York, NY). Zone 2 and Zone 3 ranges also exist but the role is based in Menlo Park or New York, so Zone 1 is treated as the primary range.
Nice-to-haves section is empty because no items in the JD are framed as preferred, bonus, or nice-to-have — all listed qualifications appear as unconditional bullets under 'What you bring'.
